skill.name: firecrawl-build-interact
skill.description: Integrate Firecrawl /interact into product code for dynamic pages, browser actions, forms, pagination, and authenticated scraping workflows.
skill.summary: Build Firecrawl integrations that use browser interaction when scraping alone cannot reach the needed content.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak crypto, filesystem traversal, network, and credential access patterns. Review of SKILL.md shows these are Markdown endpoint names, documentation links, a declared Firecrawl API key input, and relative links; no executable code, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. Residual risk is low because the skill guides network use of Firecrawl and authenticated browser interaction patterns.

low_findings.title: Markdown Endpoint Names Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: Inline references to Firecrawl endpoints use Markdown backticks, but SKILL.md contains no shell command, Ruby command substitution, or executable script. The command execution and weak crypto static hits are false positives from documentation text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose containing endpoint names such as /scrape and /interact. No executable code or shell invocation exists in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Declared Firecrawl API Key Input
low_findings.description: The skill declares FIRECRAWL_API_KEY as a required input for hosted Firecrawl requests. This is expected for the integration, but users should treat the key as sensitive and avoid exposing it in generated code or logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential input is explicit and semantically tied to Firecrawl API access. I found no evidence that the skill exfiltrates or mishandles the key.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Firecrawl homepage, source, and language-specific documentation. These links create normal network dependency for documentation lookup, not unauthorized outbound data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and source links in Markdown. There is no code path that sends local files, secrets, or user data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Relative Skill Links Misclassified as Traversal
low_findings.description: Relative paths point to neighboring Firecrawl skill documentation files. They are Markdown links, not filesystem operations, and there is no path construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal tokens appear only inside relative Markdown links. I found no file read, write, include, or dynamic path behavior in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Dynamic pages often hide data behind clicks, forms, filters, pagination, or authenticated flows. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design Firecrawl /interact integrations that act on pages before extracting data.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when Firecrawl /interact is more appropriate than /scrape.
Guides implementation for clicks, typing, filters, pagination, and multi-step page flows.
Directs users to Firecrawl language documentation for Node, Python, Rust, Java, Elixir, and REST.
Encourages narrow browser workflows that minimize unnecessary interaction scope.
Covers use cases that require authenticated state or persistent browser profiles.
limitations: It does not include executable integration code or SDK wrappers.
It requires a valid Firecrawl API key for hosted Firecrawl requests.
It depends on current Firecrawl documentation for exact API syntax.
It does not replace a full browser sandbox for open-ended automation.

use_cases.title: Extract Filtered Search Results
use_cases.description: Build a workflow that fills search fields, applies filters, and extracts the resulting data.

use_cases.title: Handle Paginated Data
use_cases.description: Use Firecrawl interaction steps when data appears across pages that require browser navigation.

use_cases.title: Access Login-Gated Dashboards
use_cases.description: Plan authenticated scraping flows that keep browser context across required steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Scrape or Interact
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page workflow and decide whether Firecrawl /scrape is enough or /interact is required. Explain the smallest viable approach.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Simple Interaction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Firecrawl /interact plan for a page that needs one click or form entry before extraction. Include setup, action sequence, and extraction goal.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Step Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Firecrawl /interact integration for filters, pagination, and result extraction. Keep the browser actions scoped and note failure cases.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Authenticated Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Firecrawl /interact design for authenticated browser state, persistent profile use, security boundaries, and when a browser sandbox would be safer.

output_examples.input: A product page only reveals pricing after choosing a region from a dropdown.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend /interact because the page must be manipulated before extraction. It would define a narrow action sequence that selects the region, waits for updated content, and extracts the visible pricing.

output_examples.input: A search page shows results after entering a query and clicking through three result pages.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline a form-fill and pagination workflow. It would keep interaction steps limited to the search box, submit action, next-page control, and result extraction.

output_examples.input: A team wants to scrape a dashboard after signing in with an existing browser profile.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag authenticated state as a reason to consider persistent profiles. It would also recommend reviewing whether a browser sandbox is a better fit for broad automation.

best_practices: Start with /scrape, then use /interact only when page actions are required.
Keep each interaction flow focused on the smallest sequence that unlocks the data.
Read the Firecrawl source-of-truth documentation for the target programming language before writing code.
anti_patterns: Using /interact for static pages that /scrape can read directly.
Creating broad browser automation flows without clear boundaries or extraction goals.
Persisting authenticated browser profiles when the task does not require session continuity.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when a Firecrawl integration must click, type, navigate, paginate, filter, or preserve browser context before extraction.

faq.question: Does this skill replace Firecrawl /scrape?
faq.answer: No. It recommends starting with /scrape and using /interact only when page actions are required.

faq.question: Does it provide complete code examples?
faq.answer: No. It points to source-of-truth Firecrawl documentation before implementation code is written.

faq.question: Does it require credentials?
faq.answer: Hosted Firecrawl requests require FIRECRAWL_API_KEY. Self-hosted deployments can also use a custom FIRECRAWL_API_URL.

faq.question: Can it help with authenticated pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers cases where persistent profiles or authenticated browser state may be required.

faq.question: Is it safe for open-ended browser automation?
faq.answer: It advises evaluating a browser sandbox when the workflow becomes open-ended rather than narrowly scoped.
